citations.narrative:
Upon inspection, it was observed that due to the design of certain exhibition areas the public had direct contact with
animals at the following exhibition areas: George's Hen House(approximately 10 chickens), Mama Goat
Pen(approximately 12 goats), East Chicken Pen (approximately 8 chickens), the Dairy Goat Pen (approximately 10
goats), Turkey Pen (approximately 5 turkeys), and Buck Pen (approximately 2 sheep). In addition, no public attendees
were observed at any of the exhibition areas listed above. At George's Hen House the inspector observed a child poking a
chicken with a stick through the exhibit fence and the chicken moved away in response to the poking. Allowing the public
direct contact with the animals without an attendant present could lead to discomfort or harm to the animal or harm to the
public.
A responsible, knowledgeable, and readily identifiable employee or attendant must be present at all times during periods
of public contact.
The facility must ensure that an attendant must be present at all times when the public has direct contact with the animals
on exhibit.
Correction Date: May 16, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative.
Additional Inspectors:
KELLY MAXWELL n

narrative:
2.131(d)(2) 
   
Handling of animals. 
Upon inspection, it was observed that due to the design of certain exhibition areas the public had direct contact with 
animals at the following exhibition areas: George's Hen House(approximately 10 chickens), Mama Goat 
Pen(approximately 12 goats), East Chicken Pen (approximately 8 chickens), the Dairy Goat Pen (approximately 10 
goats), Turkey Pen (approximately 5 turkeys), and Buck Pen (approximately 2 sheep). In addition, no public attendees 
were observed at any of the exhibition areas listed above. At George's Hen House the inspector observed a child poking a 
chicken with a stick through the exhibit fence and the chicken moved away in response to the poking. Allowing the public 
direct contact with the animals without an attendant present could lead to discomfort or harm to the animal or harm to the 
public. 
 
A responsible, knowledgeable, and readily identifiable employee or attendant must be present at all times during periods 
of public contact. 
 
The facility must ensure that an attendant must be present at all times when the public has direct contact with the animals 
on exhibit. 
 
Correction Date: May 16, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
KELLY MAXWELL n
